title: City Summer Uniform
description: Oversized tees, clean sneakers and color-first styling for hot concrete days.
mood: Heatwave graphics, court energy and soft utility accessories.

chapters.title: Start with volume.
chapters.body: The tee stays oversized and graphic so the outfit reads intentional from across the street.

chapters.title: Keep the sneaker clean but loud.
chapters.body: A cobalt court shoe keeps the palette playful without turning the full outfit into costume.

chapters.title: Add utility without weight.
chapters.body: A compact canvas tote finishes the fit and keeps the whole look city-ready.

title: Night Market Fit
description: Black graphics, redline sneakers and a hoodie layer for after-dark movement.
mood: Low light, reflective hits and pieces that move from dinner to late train rides.

chapters.title: Anchor the outfit in black.
chapters.body: A heavy graphic tee gives the look a strong base before the redline sneaker appears.

chapters.title: Zip hoodie, not bulk.
chapters.body: The hoodie works as a moving layer: open when warm, zipped when the night turns cold.

chapters.title: Let the shoe carry the hit.
chapters.body: A redline skate sneaker adds speed and contrast without fighting the darker base.

title: Gallery Runner
description: Cream runners and art tees styled like a moving exhibition.
mood: Art-school polish, clean runners and painterly graphics for quiet confidence.

chapters.title: Make the graphic feel curated.
chapters.body: Painterly color turns the tee into a gallery object instead of a generic print.

chapters.title: A cream runner keeps it light.
chapters.body: The shoe creates movement without stealing focus from the artwork on the tee.

chapters.title: Espresso pulls it together.
chapters.body: A darker atelier tee can be layered into the fit when the palette needs more depth.
